babyDevelopment: The'tail' at the base of the spinal cord has disappeared. Muscles are starting to form, allowing the fetus to make tiny, spontaneous movements (though you can't feel them yet). The digestive system is developing, and the liver is starting to produce red blood cells.
bodyChanges: Mood swings might be intense—similar to PMS but amplified. You may experience 'stuffy nose' (pregnancy rhinitis) due to increased blood flow to mucous membranes. Progesterone may cause slower digestion and bloating.
tips: Use a saline nasal spray for congestion
Stay consistent with your light exercise routine
Talk to your partner about your changing moods

do: Eat fiber-rich foods to help with digestion
Take 5-minute walks every hour if sitting long
dont: Use decongestant nasal sprays without asking doc

travelAdvisory.note: Travel is safe; stay hydrated to combat rhinitis.

milestoneDescription: Your baby is starting to make its first tiny, involuntary moves.
